ORDER SUSTAINING DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFF'S AMENDED COMPLAINT AND DISMISSING SECOND AND THIRD CAUSES OF ACTION, INCLUDING ALL CLAIMS AGAINST DEFENDANTS OUTMESGUINE AND ELLIOTT, WITH PREJUDICE
MINI-MINUTES FOR FEB-17-2009 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 301

LAW AND MOTION 301, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS ANGELIQUE BREEDLOVE, DIANA OUTMESGUINE, BETH ELLIOTT, AND BAKER & MCKENZIE LL P'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. WORKERS COMPENSATION IS THE EXCLUSIVE REMEDY TO THE THE EXTENT PLAINTIFF IS CLAIMING PERSONAL INJURY SO THE DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO OUTMESGUINE AND ELLIOT AND AS TO THE ASSAULT AND INTENTIONAL INFLICTION OF EMOTIONAL DISTRESS CLAIMS EACH OF WHICH IS IN ANY CASE DEFICIENT FOR PLEADIDNG LACK OF PHYSICAL THREAT OR OUTRAGEOUS CONDUCT. THE FRAUD CLAIM FAILS TO PLEAD JUSTIFIABLE RELIANCE OR LOSS CAUSED BY THE ALLEGED FRAUD SO IT IS SUSTAINED WITH 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO THE EXTENT PLAINTIFF CAN PLEAD THOSE ELEMENTS ARE SOME ECONOMIC INJURY IN GOOD FAITH. ORDER WAS SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL A. KAREN CSR# 8189 301/PJB
PROOF OF SERVICE OF SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT FILED BY PLAINTIFF MERRILL, CHERYL SERVED FEB-17-2009, MAIL AS TO DEFENDANT BREEDLOVE, ANGELIQUE OUTMESGUINE, DIANA ELLIOTT, BETH BAKER & MCKENZIE LLP
